core.title: WordPress Core <= 6.2 - Directory Traversal via Translation Files
core.description: WordPress Core is vulnerable to directory traversal via the 'lang' parameter allowing attackers to read files outside the intended directory.

core.title: WordPress Core <= 5.9.1 - SQL Injection via WP_Meta_Query
core.description: Authenticated SQL injection via the meta_query parameter in WP_Meta_Query.

core.title: WordPress Core <= 5.9.1 - Object Injection via Multisites
core.description: Authenticated super-admin can exploit object injection in multisites.

core.title: WordPress Core <= 5.9.1 - Authenticated XSS via Post Slugs
core.description: Authenticated low-privileged user can inject malicious scripts via post slugs.

core.title: WordPress Core <= 5.9.1 - SQL Injection via WP_Query
core.description: Unauthenticated SQL injection via the WP_Query class, exploitable through URL parameters.

core.title: WordPress 5.6-5.7 - Authenticated XXE via Media File Upload
core.description: An authenticated user with the ability to upload files can trigger XXE via crafted WAV/OGG files.

core.title: WordPress 3.7-5.7 - Password-Protected Posts Exposure
core.description: Block-based themes may expose content of password-protected posts to unauthenticated users.

core.title: WordPress <= 5.4.1 - Privilege Escalation via set-screen-option
core.description: Misuse of set-screen-option filters allows authenticated users to escalate privileges.

core.title: WordPress <= 5.2.3 - Unauthenticated View Private/Draft Posts
core.description: Unauthenticated users can view private and draft posts in certain configurations.

core.title: WordPress < 5.1.1 - Cross-Site Request Forgery in Comment Handling
core.description: CSRF allows remote attackers to perform actions on behalf of authenticated users via comment manipulation.

core.title: WordPress <= 4.9.8 - Authenticated File Delete (Arbitrary File Deletion)
core.description: Authenticated users can delete arbitrary files, including wp-config.php.

core.title: WordPress < 4.7.5 - SSRF via HTTP class
core.description: Server-Side Request Forgery via the HTTP class allows attackers to make requests to internal services.

contact-form-7.title: Contact Form 7 < 5.8.4 - Unrestricted File Upload leading to RCE
contact-form-7.description: Lack of file type validation allows uploading PHP files via the file attachment field.

contact-form-7.title: Contact Form 7 < 5.3.2 - Unrestricted File Upload
contact-form-7.description: Insufficient file type validation allows uploading arbitrary files.

woocommerce.title: WooCommerce < 5.5.1 - SQL Injection via Order Search
woocommerce.description: Authenticated SQL injection via the order search functionality in WooCommerce admin.

woocommerce.title: WooCommerce < 6.6.0 - Stored XSS via Coupon Title
woocommerce.description: Stored XSS vulnerability in WooCommerce coupon title field.

elementor.title: Elementor < 3.6.3 - Remote Code Execution via Log File Upload
elementor.description: Authenticated subscribers can execute arbitrary PHP code via crafted log file uploads.

elementor.title: Elementor <= 3.18.0 - Arbitrary File Upload
elementor.description: Authenticated contributors can upload arbitrary files via Elementor template import.

yoast-seo.title: Yoast SEO < 17.3 - Reflected XSS via Indexables Parameter
yoast-seo.description: Reflected XSS via the 'type' parameter in the Indexables section.

all-in-one-seo-pack.title: All In One SEO < 4.1.5.3 - Privilege Escalation & SQLi
all-in-one-seo-pack.description: Authenticated subscribers can escalate privileges to admin and perform SQL injection.

wp-super-cache.title: WP Super Cache < 1.7.2 - RCE via Malicious Settings
wp-super-cache.description: Authenticated admins can execute arbitrary code via crafted cache settings.

buddypress.title: BuddyPress < 7.2.1 - Privilege Escalation (REST API)
buddypress.description: Authenticated subscribers can escalate to admin via the BuddyPress REST API.

bbpress.title: bbPress < 2.6.5 - Unauthenticated Privilege Escalation
bbpress.description: Unauthenticated users can register as administrators due to improper role assignment.

wordfence.title: Wordfence < 7.9.2 - Bypass via IP Spoofing
wordfence.description: IP spoofing via X-Forwarded-For header allows bypassing IP-based blocking rules.

jetpack.title: Jetpack < 12.1.1 - Reflected XSS in Contact Form
jetpack.description: Reflected XSS in the Jetpack contact form via unvalidated callback parameters.

wp-file-manager.title: WP File Manager < 6.9 - Unauthenticated RCE
wp-file-manager.description: Unauthenticated remote code execution via elFinder file manager plugin.

duplicator.title: Duplicator < 1.3.28 - Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Download
duplicator.description: Unauthenticated users can download arbitrary files including wp-config.php.

wpforms-lite.title: WPForms Lite < 1.6.8 - CSRF to Stored XSS
wpforms-lite.description: CSRF vulnerability allows attackers to inject persistent XSS via form submissions.

loginpress.title: LoginPress < 1.6.6 - Authentication Bypass
loginpress.description: Unauthenticated attackers can bypass authentication and log in as any user.

advanced-custom-fields.title: Advanced Custom Fields < 6.1.6 - Reflected XSS
advanced-custom-fields.description: Unauthenticated reflected XSS via the admin panel ACF field parameters.

updraftplus.title: UpdraftPlus < 1.22.3 - Sensitive Data Exposure
updraftplus.description: Subscriber-level users can download backup files containing sensitive credentials.

w3-total-cache.title: W3 Total Cache < 2.1.5 - Server-Side Request Forgery
w3-total-cache.description: Authenticated SSRF via the CDN configuration endpoint.

the-events-calendar.title: The Events Calendar < 5.12.4 - SQL Injection
the-events-calendar.description: Unauthenticated SQL injection via the 'search' parameter in the REST API.

give.title: Give < 2.17.3 - SQL Injection via Donor ID
give.description: Authenticated admin SQL injection via the donor_id parameter.

popup-maker.title: Popup Maker < 1.18.2 - Unauthenticated Stored XSS
popup-maker.description: Unauthenticated stored XSS via the popup form submission functionality.

easy-digital-downloads.title: Easy Digital Downloads < 2.11.6 - Subscriber+ CSRF & XSS
easy-digital-downloads.description: CSRF combined with XSS allows privilege escalation for subscriber-level users.

twentytwenty.title: Twenty Twenty <= 1.9 - Reflected XSS via Search
twentytwenty.description: Reflected XSS via the search parameter in the theme's search template.

divi.title: Divi < 4.14.7 - Reflected XSS via Multiple Parameters
divi.description: Multiple reflected XSS vulnerabilities via theme option parameters.

flatsome.title: Flatsome < 3.17.5 - Stored XSS via Product Description
flatsome.description: Authenticated stored XSS via WooCommerce product descriptions in the Flatsome theme.
